channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Suspension lifted for helicopter pilots who hovered near Kid Rock's home
item.description: The Army pilots who hovered two helicopters near Kid Rock's Tennessee home during a training run while he clapped and saluted have had their suspension lifted, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
The Army pilots who hovered two helicopters near Kid Rock's Tennessee home during a training run while he clapped and saluted have had their suspension lifted, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tuesday.

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump plans to attend Wednesday's Supreme Court hearing on birthright citizenship
item.description: President Donald Trump plans to sit in on Wednesday's Supreme Court hearing on birthright citizenship, making him the first sitting president to attend oral arguments at the nation's highest court.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump plans to sit in on Wednesday's Supreme Court hearing on birthright citizenship, making him the first sitting president to attend oral arguments at the nation's highest court.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Tiger Woods says he'll seek treatment after pleading not guilty to DUI
item.description: Woods said Tuesday he is stepping away to seek treatment, four days after his vehicle crashed in Florida and he was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence. He will miss the Masters for the second straight year.
item.content:encoded:
Woods said Tuesday he is stepping away to seek treatment, four days after his vehicle crashed in Florida and he was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence. He will miss the Masters for the second straight year.

(Image credit: Jason Oteri)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Here to help: These volunteers build and maintain hiking trails in North Carolina
item.description: When you hike or ride a bike in Charlotte, N.C., it's likely you're on a path built and maintained by volunteers. We'll meet them in our series Here to Help.
item.content:encoded: When you hike or ride a bike in Charlotte, N.C., it's likely you're on a path built and maintained by volunteers. We'll meet them in our series Here to Help.
item.dc:creator: Eric Teel

item.title: Runners misled at a qualifier race will still get invites to the world championship
item.description: The U.S. will nearly double its contingent for the women's half marathon championship to fix what officials call an unprecedented problem: an official vehicle took the leading runners off the course.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. will nearly double its contingent for the women's half marathon championship to fix what officials call an unprecedented problem: an official vehicle took the leading runners off the course.

(Image credit: Matthew Demarko via Atlanta Track Club)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Federal judge finds Trump violated free speech by ordering NPR defunded
item.description: A U.S. District Court judge found that President Trump's executive order calling for the defunding of NPR and PBS violated the First Amendment.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. District Court judge found that President Trump's executive order calling for the defunding of NPR and PBS violated the First Amendment.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Trump shares a look at his future presidential library. Here's what to know about it
item.description: Trump posted the first architectural renderings of his future presidential library, planned for a prime plot of land donated by Miami Dade College.
item.content:encoded: Trump posted the first architectural renderings of his future presidential library, planned for a prime plot of land donated by Miami Dade College.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Supreme Court opens door to controversial conversion therapy
item.description: The Supreme Court has ruled that Colorado's law banning conversion therapy "regulates speech based on viewpoint."
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court has ruled that Colorado's law banning conversion therapy "regulates speech based on viewpoint."

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Trump hints at an end to military action in Iran, saying U.S. will leave in 2-3 weeks
item.description: Trump's remarks came in response to a question about rising gas prices. He said they would fall once the U.S. leaves Iran, claiming that would happen in "maybe two weeks, maybe three."
item.content:encoded:
Trump's remarks came in response to a question about rising gas prices. He said they would fall once the U.S. leaves Iran, claiming that would happen in "maybe two weeks, maybe three."

(Image credit: Leo Correa)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: In a town close to the farmworker movement, some struggle to process Chavez allegations
item.description: March 31 is Cesar Chavez's birthday, and a longtime holiday. In the wake of sexual assault allegations against him, residents in the farming town of Delano are conflicted about how to remember him.
item.content:encoded:
March 31 is Cesar Chavez's birthday, and a longtime holiday. In the wake of sexual assault allegations against him, residents in the farming town of Delano are conflicted about how to remember him.

(Image credit: Jennifer Emerling for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Kerry Klein
